title: now_millis()/now_secs() use .unwrap() on SystemTime without justification
description: api.rs lines 29-33 and 35-40: now_millis() and now_secs() both call SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).unwrap(). While this only panics if the system clock is before 1970 (extremely unlikely), there is no comment justifying the unwrap. What goes wrong: if the system clock is broken (NTP glitch, VM snapshot restore), the server panics instead of degrading gracefully. Low probability but the fix is trivial — add a // SAFETY comment explaining the precondition, or use .expect("system clock before UNIX_EPOCH") for a clear panic message.
